THE CITY MANAGER OF ELLIS REPORTED MULTIPLE BUILDINGS DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS BUILDINGS PARTIALLY DAMAGED IN ELLIS. 40 PERCENT OF THE HEADSTONES IN THE CEMETARY WERE TURNED OVER. BIG TREES WERE SNAPPED AND SEVERAL FENCES WERE DOWN.
8 INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN RUSH CENTER.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED WIND GUSTS AVERAGING BETWEEN 60 AND 65 MPH.
HAIL WAS FALLING AT MILE MARKER 135 ON I-70.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AT SEVERAL FARMS IN NORTHWESTERN ELLIS COUNTY. ONE DRIVEWAY WAS BLOCKED DUE TO TREES THAT HAD BEEN BLOWN DOWN. THE FARMS ALSO LOST POWER.
HAIL STARTED OUT AS GOLFBALL, THEN BECAME QUARTER SIZED. ALL WINDOWS IN A FARM HOUSE BLOWN OUT. SEVERAL WHEAT FIELDS HAVE BEEN DESTROYED.
DISPATCHER ADVISED 60 PLUS MPH WINDS AND BREAKING TREE LIMBS.
A CARPORT WAS BLOWN DOWN.
THE WINDS BLEW DOWN TREE BRANCHES THAT WERE 2-3 INCHES IN DIAMETER.

THE STANTON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER INITIALLY REPORTED POWER POLES DOWN IN THE COUNTY DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS. LAW ENFORCEMENT CONFIRMED POWER POLES DOWN WITHIN THE CITY OF JOHNSON AT 835PM AND POWER POLES DOWN FOUR MILES WEST OF JOHNSON REPORTED LATER AT 848PM.
A 300 LB DOGHOUSE WAS ALSO BLOWN OVER.
THE SEWARD COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED DAMAGE TO A ROOF OF A FARM SHED.
THE SEWARD COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A MORTON BUILDING BLOWN DOWN IN TOWN NEAR THE VICINITY OF SEVENTH STREET AND INDUSTRIAL AVENUE.
THE SEWARD COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A STORM SPOTTER ESTIMATING 70 MPH WIND GUSTS IN TOWN AND MULTIPLE POWER LINES DOWN.
THE SEWARD COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THAT A PARTIALLY DEAD TREE WAS BLOWN OVER ONTO A HOME THAT DAMAGED THE PORCH ROOF.
SPORADIC GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED.
SPORADIC GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED ALONG WITH SMALLER HAIL. THE SMALLER HAIL FELL FOR ABOUT 3 HOURS FROM ABOUT 530 PM TILL 830 PM.

A TREE WAS BLOWN ONTO A HOUSE BY THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS.
A FEW 1-INCH LIVE TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN. A TRAMPOLINE IN A PROTECTED AREA WAS BLOWN AND FLIPPED OVER, IT HAD NEVER BEEN MOVED BEFORE. ALSO, A SECTION OF CORN WAS LAYING FLAT FROM WIND AND RAIN.
STRONG WINDS BLEW A PIVOT SPRINKLER SYSTEM INTO A ROAD, SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE SAME ROAD, O-ROAD. ALSO, A 30X10 FOOT HORSE BARN WAS BLOWN OVER A FENCE AND DAMAGED.
REPORT IS FROM APRSWXNET.
REPORT IS FROM APRSWXNET.

SIX TO SEVEN POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN.

TWO TO THREE INCH DIAMETER LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN.

IT WAS ALSO RAINING VERY HARD AT THE TIME OF THE CALL.
IT WAS ALSO RAINING VERY HARD AT THE TIME OF THE CALL AND POWER WAS OUT AT THE CALLER/S FARM.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A BACKYARD FENCE BLOWN HALF WAY OVER AS A RESULT OF A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST AT AROUND 1030PM. THE WIND GUST WAS ESTIMATED AT AROUND 70 MPH.
THE TOP HALF OF AN 8-INCH DIAMETER TREE WAS SNAPPED OFF. THE TIME WAS ESTIMATED.
A TRAMPOLINE WITH A WIRE CAGE WAS FLIPPED OVER AND BLOWN ABOUT 90 YARDS AWAY. ALSO, TWO 15-FOOT PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE. THE ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 65 MPH.

DAMAGE AT THE HERITAGE FEEDERS WAS REPORTED. MANY TALL STANDING STRUCTURES SUFFERED SOME SORT OF DAMAGE INCLUDING THE FEED MILL...CONCRETE SILO...CONCRETE WALLS WHICH LEANED AGAINST PILLARS...AND SOME SHEDS. GUSTS IN EXCESS OF 80 MPH WERE LIKELY EXPERIENCED HERE.
